title: myriophyllum pedunculatum subsp. novae-zelandiae
intro: Myriophyllum pedunculatum subsp. novae-zelandiae, also known as New Zealand water milfoil, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of various health issues. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. New Zealand water milfoil can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
